mechanism_narrative: A positive three-month change in real retail sales (RSAFS) signals robust consumer demand that translates into higher corporate revenues, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following positive 3-month RSAFS changes fail to exceed the unconditional positive baseline rate of 65.5% by at least 5 percentage points over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) of at least 0.3 percentage points over three months signals accelerating labor market deterioration that precedes downward earnings revisions, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are not negative in at least 55% of instances when the UNRATE trigger condition is met over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) and a federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) above 3.5 percent jointly indicate a restrictive monetary policy drag on the real economy, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between INDPRO momentum and FEDFUNDS level produces a Rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or joint-signal periods fail to show negative 20-day returns in > 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary easing cycles, stronger-than-trend real GDP growth (GDPC1) indicates successful policy transmission into aggregate demand, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In identified easing cycles, positive GDPC1 surprises fail to produce average 60-day SPX returns that exceed the unconditional baseline by 2 annualized percentage points over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) that exceeds its trailing six-month average monthly change signals an inflation surprise that prompts discount-rate repricing, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 1-month change exceeds trailing 6-month average 1-month change
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following CPIAUCSL acceleration triggers are negative in fewer than 55% of triggered instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month decline in housing starts (HOUST) forecasts weakness in construction activity and related consumer durables spending, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns following negative 3-month HOUST momentum are not negative in > 55% of triggered instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Higher levels of the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) increase the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, causing lower S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Spearman rank correlation between FEDFUNDS level and subsequent 60-day SPX returns is not negative with a one-sided p-value below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Negative month-over-month nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth signals immediate labor market contraction that reduces household income expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns following negative month-over-month PAYEMS changes are not negative in > 55% of triggered instances over any 2-year rolling window.
